patch.value: Sentry PM-CLI-2G: make merge-driver installation permission failures actionable

Sentry reports a handled high-severity production failure when pm merge install attempts to configure the repository-local merge driver in a workspace where .git/config cannot be locked. Harden the merge-install boundary so expected read-only or permission-constrained repositories return a deterministic, actionable pm error instead of surfacing a raw child-process failure, while preserving normal field-aware merge-driver installation.
production CI and permission-constrained Git workspaces
Run pm merge install in a valid Git worktree where repository-local Git configuration cannot be written.
The command fails with a stable pm error code and remediation guidance, classifies the expected permission constraint appropriately in telemetry, and leaves Git configuration unchanged.
The underlying git config command fails with 'could not lock config file .git/config: Permission denied' and produces handled high-severity Sentry issue PM-CLI-2G.
Agents in read-only CI or sandboxed worktrees receive a raw infrastructure error and observability records an expected environmental constraint as a high-severity product failure.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check: searched all statuses for PM-CLI-2G, merge.pm-item-toon, git config, merge install, and permission denied; no item tracked this exact failure. Live Sentry evidence: PM-CLI-2G is new, unresolved, handled, count 2, first seen 2026-07-21T21:37:22.513Z and last seen 2026-07-21T21:37:23.120Z in @unbrained/pm-cli 2026.7.21. value.author: codex-root
value.text: Acceptance scope: cover a real temporary Git repository with a deliberately non-writable repository config boundary; assert deterministic error metadata and remediation text; prove no partial merge-driver configuration; preserve successful installation and idempotency; verify telemetry/Sentry classification for expected permission constraints; update merge-safety documentation.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Permission-constrained Git metadata is a normal execution environment for CI and review sandboxes. Merge-driver setup should distinguish an expected environmental inability to mutate local Git config from an unexpected application defect.
